name: Northern Mariana Islands (United States)

colors.symbolism: the Pacific Ocean and the sky
the Latte stone, the star, and peace
the stone of the Latte and the wreath
the mwar (head garland) wreath of plumeria, ylang-ylang, and other native flowers

description: A dark blue field with a centred white five-pointed star superimposed on a grey Latte stone (a traditional Chamorro stone pillar with a hemispherical capstone), all encircled by a green mwar wreath of plumeria, hibiscus, and other native flowers.
history: Originally adopted on 4 July 1976 with just the Latte stone and star. The mwar floral wreath was added on 1 July 1989 to better incorporate Carolinian heritage alongside Chamorro symbols, after the Northern Mariana Islands' transition from a UN Trust Territory to a United States Commonwealth in 1978.

features.symbolism: the deep blue of the Pacific Ocean

the traditional Chamorro Latte stone, an ancient pillar of cut limestone topped by a hemispherical capstone, used as a foundation for Chamorro houses; symbolizes the indigenous Chamorro and Carolinian heritage of the islands.

features.role: white five-pointed star centred over the Latte stone
features.symbolism: the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

features.role: mwar (floral head wreath) encircling the Latte stone and star
features.symbolism: the floral wreath traditionally worn by Chamorros and Carolinians; signifies cultural pride and the unity of the islanders.
